title: Prismatomeris beccariana
taxonomy: Prismatomeris beccariana is classified within the family Rubiaceae, a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the madder or coffee family due to some of its well-known members. Within the Rubiaceae family, Prismatomeris beccariana is placed in the subfamily Cinchonoideae. Prismatomeris beccariana is further classified within the tribe Naucleeae, which consists of several species found in tropical regions. The exact classification of Prismatomeris beccariana can be subject to further refinement as botanical classification evolves.

intro: Prismatomeris beccariana is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the Beccarian prismatomeris. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of P. beccariana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as prismatomerin and isoprasin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, P. beccariana is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
